title: podman run uses a different image than what was just built by podman build
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If I build a container using `podman build` and give it a name like "d", often running the container runs a different container than the just-built one. It seems like the name stays associated with some other container image?

This bug doesn't always happen for me, but when it does, it's repeatable: I can repeat the same build commands and run commands and the wrong container is consistently used for the same build/run commands.
 
Cleaning all images generally fixes the issue, until it re-appears a few days/weeks later.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a `Dockerfile`. This one is using `FROM quay.io/centos/centos:stream9-development`.
2. Build it:  `podman build -t d -f Dockerfile`
2. Run the image and show the OS version: `podman run -it d /usr/bin/cat /etc/os-release`. Notice it prints the wrong OS version. It's not CentOS Stream 9 like I expected based on the `FROM` line.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman build -t d -f src/centos/stream9/Dockerfile
STEP 1/2: FROM quay.io/centos/centos:stream9-development
STEP 2/2: RUN dnf install --setopt tsflags=nodocs --refresh -y         dnf-plugins-core     &&     dnf config-manager --set-enabled crb     &&     dnf install --setopt tsflags=nodocs -y         "perl(Time::HiRes)"         autoconf         automake         clang         cmake         curl-devel         doxygen         findutils         gcc         gdb         git         glibc-langpack-en         hostname         krb5-devel         libcurl-devel         libedit-devel         libicu-devel         libidn2-devel         libnghttp2-devel         libtool         libuuid-devel         libxml2-devel         lld         lldb-devel         llvm         lttng-ust-devel         lzma         make         ncurses-devel         numactl-devel         openssl-devel         procps-ng         python3         python3-devel         readline-devel         sudo         swig         tar         wget         which         xz         zlib-devel     &&     dnf clean all
--> Using cache e8806dd63c4d34c881d03b7a4fad0690e2390fe0255bf2948855252cbf38fd29
COMMIT d
--> e8806dd63c4
Successfully tagged localhost/d:latest
e8806dd63c4d34c881d03b7a4fad0690e2390fe0255bf2948855252cbf38fd29
$ podman run -it d /usr/bin/cat /etc/os-release
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="36 (Container Image Prerelease)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=36
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f36"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora Linux 36 (Container Image Prerelease)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:36"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/rawhide/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://ask.fedoraproject.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=rawhide
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=rawhide
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Container Image"
VARIANT_ID=container
$ podman images --all
REPOSITORY                         TAG                  IMAGE ID      CREATED            SIZE
localhost/d                        latest               e8806dd63c4d  About an hour ago  1.36 GB
quay.io/centos/centos              stream9-development  a0139f353193  14 hours ago       146 MB
quay.io/centos/centos              stream9              b575d3c08f6e  2 weeks ago        152 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8    latest               fca12da1dc30  3 weeks ago        235 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  rawhide              dfe12cab192e  3 weeks ago        166 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  35                   3059bef432eb  6 weeks ago        159 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  34                   b0e38cd3ecc2  6 weeks ago        184 MB
quay.io/centos/centos              8                    5d0da3dc9764  3 months ago       239 MB
registry.centos.org/centos         8                    e64b6e20a604  13 months ago      217 MB

```

**Describe the results you expected:**

`podman run` would run the just-built container image, not some other image.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- I don't have a perfect way to reproduce this, this only happens sometimes. But once it does, repeating the build and run commands doesn't fix it.
- Using a `localhost/` prefix  (like `localhost/d` in the example) seems to use the right container image.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 16:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug                                                                     [43/2120]
host:                   
  arch: amd64          
  buildahVersion: 1.23.1

      host_id: 1000              
      size: 1

      host_id: 100000            
      size: 65536                                                              
  kernel: 5.15.11-200.fc35.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic                  
  logDriver: journald
  memFree: 2627309568
  memTotal: 33391194112
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-1.3-1.fc35.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.3
      commit: 8e5757a4e68590326dafe8a8b1b4a584b10a1370
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: true
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:

      commit: 7a104a101aa3278a2152351a082a6df71f57c9a3
      libslirp: 4.6.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.3
  swapFree: 8431071232
  swapTotal: 8589930496
  uptime: 50h 54m 28.46s (Approximately 2.08 days)

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ rpm -q podman 
podman-3.4.4-1.fc35.x86_64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

A physical x86_64 machine running Fedora 35. Problem also existed in older Fedora versions (and older podman versions, I assume).

comments.body:
Thank you for reaching out!  Are there other services running on your machine which may be using the same image `d`?

The reported randomness in reproducibility suggests a race and I am fairly sure there is none in the code.

I am running into this again today and I don't see any duplicate images. Today, I am using `f`:

```
$ podman build -t f -f dotnet6.0-fedora.dockerfile
STEP 1/2: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:35
STEP 2/2: RUN dnf install -yq       clang       cmake       coreutils       findutils       git       glibc-langpack-en       hostname       krb5-devel       libicu-devel       libunwind-devel       lld       llvm       lttng-ust-devel       make       openssl-devel       python3       tar       util-linux       zlib-devel
--> Using cache a6a00f7191e0e1daa8480f6011c5f2c13985a8108cc48c45f2942f0f3db2ef1a
COMMIT f
--> a6a00f7191e
Successfully tagged localhost/f:latest
a6a00f7191e0e1daa8480f6011c5f2c13985a8108cc48c45f2942
$ podman run -it f /bin/bash
[root@4d7352ec1965 /]# git
bash: git: command not found
[root@4d7352ec1965 /]# exit
```

I built a Fedora container, including `git` and `git` isn't found here. Looks like it's using another container instead:

```
$ podman ps -a | grep 4d7352ec1965
4d7352ec1965  registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8:latest     /bin/bash             About a minute ago  Exited (127) About a minute ago              heuristic_rhodes
```

I don't have any other containers running, at least that I can see via `podman ps`:

```
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
$ podman images | grep 'f'
localhost/f                        latest               a6a00f7191e0  17 minutes ago  1.11 GB
localhost/a                        latest               af996b398a90  7 hours ago     563 MB
<none>                             <none>               019e65ff9940  7 hours ago     563 MB
<none>                             <none>               a372f153b124  4 days ago      703 MB
<none>                             <none>               8bc299f09457  4 days ago      702 MB
<none>                             <none>               0ce80fa66c94  4 days ago      702 MB
quay.io/centos/centos              stream9-development  a0139f353193  12 days ago     146 MB
quay.io/centos/centos              stream9              b575d3c08f6e  4 weeks ago     152 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8    latest               fca12da1dc30  5 weeks ago     235 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  rawhide              dfe12cab192e  5 weeks ago     166 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  36                   dfe12cab192e  5 weeks ago     166 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  35                   3059bef432eb  7 weeks ago     159 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora  34                   b0e38cd3ecc2  7 weeks ago     184 MB
docker.io/library/alpine           latest               c059bfaa849c  7 weeks ago     5.87 MB
```
Could you inspect your latest container and make sure it is built on  image a6a00f7191e0

You could also
```
$ podman unshare
# mnt=$(podman image mount f)
# ls $/mnt/bin/git
```

> Could you inspect your latest container and make sure it is built on image a6a00f7191e0

What's the right way to do this?

> You could also ...

I have a few other workarounds too. I would really like to understand the bug, though (and hopefully get it resolved too).

> What's the right way to do this?
```
podman inspect image-id
```
Check the ID part in the output.

```
$ podman inspect f
Error: more than one result for container ID f: container already exists
```

That seems a bit weird can you try
```
podman image inspect name/id
```
Do you have any container with the same name? Try the ID instead

```
podman container ls -a
```

`podman inspect f` is working today, so I don't know if I can provide anything useful. 

What data should I collect when I next run into this error?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@vrothberg This looks like an error in containers/common/libimage.

We should default this to look at localhost/d

It looks like the name expansion of images is somehow effecting this.

I got this to fail and see this behavior.
```
$ podman run localhost/d cat /etc/redhat-release 
CentOS Stream release 9
$ podman run d cat /etc/redhat-release 
Fedora release 35 (Thirty Five)
 $ podman images
REPOSITORY                                TAG                  IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
localhost/d                               latest               fe057e91ed62  17 hours ago  146 MB
quay.io/centos/centos                     stream9-development  fe057e91ed62  17 hours ago  146 MB
quay.io/podman/stable                     latest               d257063ef809  2 days ago    360 MB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora         latest               e417cd49a84e  12 days ago   159 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/httpd-24  latest               028796994dbd  3 weeks ago   470 MB
registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8           latest               b81e86a2cb9a  3 weeks ago   235 MB
docker.io/library/alpine                  latest               c059bfaa849c  2 months ago  5.87 MB
quay.io/libpod/testimage                  20210610             9f9ec7f2fdef  8 months ago  7.99 MB
```
So the d is grabbing the fedora image which has IMAGE ID "d257063ef809"

As long as there is no clean reproducer, there is nothing I can do.

If possible, could you rerun with `podman --log-level=debug`? This will give use the logs for looking up the image in the local storage.
https://github.com/containers/common/pull/939 merged. The fix will make it into Podman 4.1.
